title: Implement Stack using Queue (using single queue)

content.problemStatement:
Implement a Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) stack using a single queue. The implemented stack should support the following operations: push, pop, top, and isEmpty.



Implement the QueueStack class:
**push**(int x): Pushes element x onto the stack.
**pop**(): Removes and returns the top element of the stack.
**top**(): Returns the top element of the stack without removing it.
**isEmpty**(): Returns true if the stack is empty, false otherwise.
constraints: The queue will not be empty when pop or top is called
content.inputFormat: The input will contain a list of commands and a list of inputs. The commands will be one of the following: push, pop, top, isEmpty. The inputs will be a list of integers.
content.outputFormat: The output will be a list of results for each command. For push, the result will be null. For pop and top, the result will be the integer value. For isEmpty, the result will be a boolean value.

solution.approach: Use a single queue to implement a stack. When pushing an element, add it to the queue and then move all existing elements to the back of the queue. When popping an element, remove the front element from the queue.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) for push, O(1) for pop, top, and isEmpty
